attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

9	
10	The `kira-gpt` Supabase Edge Function was rewritten to use LangChain (`npm:@langchain/openai`, `npm:@langchain/core/messages`) but it crashes on startup in Supabase's Deno runtime because LangChain has internal Node.js-specific dependencies (`node:fs`, `node:path`, `node:crypto`) that are not fully supported in the Supabase Edge Functions Deno environment. Initialising `new ChatOpenAI()` and `new OpenAIEmbeddings()` at module level makes this a hard crash before any request is handled.
11	
12	**Symptom:** CORS preflight (OPTIONS) returns 500 → browser blocks all POST requests → Kira shows no response.
13	
14	**Why:** `npm:` specifiers for LangChain don't work reliably in Supabase's restricted Deno runtime. The function crashes before `serve()` runs.
15	
16	**Fix plan (do tomorrow morning):**
17	1. Remove `npm:@langchain/openai` and `npm:@langchain/core/messages` imports from `supabase/functions/kira-gpt/index.ts`
18	2. Keep the LangChain memory *architecture* (vector memories, conversation summaries, fire-and-forget extraction) but implement it using raw `fetch()` calls to OpenAI API endpoints directly:
19	   - Embeddings: `POST https://api.openai.com/v1/embeddings` with model `text-embedding-3-small`
20	   - Memory extraction + summary: `POST https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions` (non-streaming, small token budget)
21	   - Main streaming response: keep existing SSE pump pattern (already proven to work)
22	3. Update `supabase/functions/kira-gpt/deno.json` to remove `@langchain/openai` and `@langchain/core` from imports
23	4. Re-deploy the function
24	
25	**Files to change:**
26	- `supabase/functions/kira-gpt/index.ts` — full rewrite keeping same logic, raw fetch() instead of LangChain classes
27	- `supabase/functions/kira-gpt/deno.json` — revert to only `@supabase/functions-js`
28	
29	**Why:** Supabase Deno runtime is restrictive — use raw fetch() for OpenAI calls instead of npm: LangChain packages.
30	**How to apply:** When resuming Kira work tomorrow, do this fix first before anything else. The migration (kira_memories table, match_kira_memories RPC, conversation_summaries) is already applied to prod — only the edge function code needs fixing.
31

file.content:
---
name: project-kira-langchain-fix
description: "LangChain npm: imports crash kira-gpt edge function on startup, blocking CORS and breaking Kira — needs fix"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: d8b100ac-5708-4187-885e-9da4729eb0fc
---

The `kira-gpt` Supabase Edge Function was rewritten to use LangChain (`npm:@langchain/openai`, `npm:@langchain/core/messages`) but it crashes on startup in Supabase's Deno runtime because LangChain has internal Node.js-specific dependencies (`node:fs`, `node:path`, `node:crypto`) that are not fully supported in the Supabase Edge Functions Deno environment. Initialising `new ChatOpenAI()` and `new OpenAIEmbeddings()` at module level makes this a hard crash before any request is handled.

**Symptom:** CORS preflight (OPTIONS) returns 500 → browser blocks all POST requests → Kira shows no response.

**Why:** `npm:` specifiers for LangChain don't work reliably in Supabase's restricted Deno runtime. The function crashes before `serve()` runs.

**Fix plan (do tomorrow morning):**
1. Remove `npm:@langchain/openai` and `npm:@langchain/core/messages` imports from `supabase/functions/kira-gpt/index.ts`
2. Keep the LangChain memory *architecture* (vector memories, conversation summaries, fire-and-forget extraction) but implement it using raw `fetch()` calls to OpenAI API endpoints directly:
   - Embeddings: `POST https://api.openai.com/v1/embeddings` with model `text-embedding-3-small`
   - Memory extraction + summary: `POST https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions` (non-streaming, small token budget)
   - Main streaming response: keep existing SSE pump pattern (already proven to work)
3. Update `supabase/functions/kira-gpt/deno.json` to remove `@langchain/openai` and `@langchain/core` from imports
4. Re-deploy the function

**Files to change:**
- `supabase/functions/kira-gpt/index.ts` — full rewrite keeping same logic, raw fetch() instead of LangChain classes
- `supabase/functions/kira-gpt/deno.json` — revert to only `@supabase/functions-js`

**Why:** Supabase Deno runtime is restrictive — use raw fetch() for OpenAI calls instead of npm: LangChain packages.
**How to apply:** When resuming Kira work tomorrow, do this fix first before anything else. The migration (kira_memories table, match_kira_memories RPC, conversation_summaries) is already applied to prod — only the edge function code needs fixing.

Good — I have the full picture. The edge function is already fixed and in good shape. The frontend is feature-rich: streaming, memory, projects, chat management, etc.

What do you want to work on? For example:
- **New feature** (file uploads that actually send content to OpenAI, image analysis, etc.)
- **Model upgrade** (swap `gpt-4o-mini` → `gpt-4o` or similar)
- **Kira settings** improvements (`KiraSettingsPanel`)
- **Quick actions** or the empty-state experience

content.text: Before I start coding, I have two quick questions to make sure I build this right.

questions.question: For "Use project" in the + menu — what should happen when clicked?

options.label: Inline project picker (Recommended)
options.description: Show your projects as a sub-menu so you can pick one without leaving the current chat. Sets the active project context for the conversation.

options.label: Keep current behavior
options.description: Clicking navigates to the Projects view as it does now — user selects a project there.

questions.question: For file uploads — PDF/DOC parsing requires a heavy browser library. Should we support them, or focus on images + .txt files only?

options.label: Images + .txt only (Recommended)
options.description: Full support for images (vision AI) and plain text files. PDFs/DOCs are out of scope for now — show a clear error if user tries.

options.label: Include PDF support
options.description: Add pdfjs-dist to parse PDFs in the browser and extract text. Heavier bundle, more setup.

- **Files (.txt)**: read as text → send as context
- **Use project**: inline sub-menu picker without leaving chat
- PDFs/DOCs: show a clear error

Starting with the edge function, then the frontend.

content.text: Clean. Let me also do a quick sanity check that the key changes are all in place:

input.description: Verify key changes are wired up

content.text:
Everything is wired up correctly and TypeScript passes. Here's what was done:

**Edge function (`kira-gpt/index.ts`):**
- Raised content-length limit to 1.2MB to allow base64 images through
- Accepts `fileContent` + `fileType` from request body
- Validates image data URLs, caps text files at 6000 chars
- For images: sends a vision message `[{type:"text",...}, {type:"image_url",...}]` to OpenAI — uses GPT-4o-mini's built-in vision support
- For text files: prepends the file content as context before the user's prompt

**Frontend (`Kira.tsx`):**
- Split into two file inputs (`imageInputRef` / `textInputRef`) with correct `accept` attributes — browser file picker now filters appropriately
- `handleFileSelect`: images get canvas-resized to max 1024px and JPEG-compressed (0.85) before being stored as base64; `.txt` files are read as text (truncated to 6000 chars); anything else gets a clear error toast
- Attachment preview now shows a thumbnail for images
- File content is captured and cleared atomically before the send
- `streamKiraResponse` now accepts and forwards `attachContent` + `attachType`
- **"Use project"** is now an inline sub-menu: lists all your projects with a checkmark on the active one, "Remove project" at the top when one is set, and a "New project" shortcut at the bottom — no more navigating away

message.content: we are done with Kira. back to mobile view.......the texts of the app in crevia are generally are to big in mobile both android and ios users....fix this only and pls dont use too much tokens

content.text: Let me check the global CSS and Tailwind config — a single base font-size fix will cascade everywhere.

input.description: Find global CSS and Tailwind config

content.text: Good. The cleanest fix is a single mobile base font-size override in `index.css`. Browsers default to 16px — dropping it to 14px on mobile scales all `rem`-based text proportionally with zero component changes.

lastPrompt: we are done with Kira. back to mobile view.......the texts of the app in crevia are generally are to big in mobile both android and ios users....fix this only and pls dont use too much tokens

content.content: The file /home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/index.css has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: +/* Scale down base font on mobile so all rem-based text is proportionally smaller */
/* Definition of the design system. All colors, gradients, fonts, etc should be defined here.

content.text:
That's it. One change, zero component edits needed. 

**What this does:** drops the browser's default `16px` root font size to `14px` on screens under 768px (all phones). Since Tailwind's text utilities (`text-sm`, `text-base`, `text-lg`, etc.) and most spacings use `rem`, everything scales down proportionally — headings, body copy, buttons, labels, all of it — without touching individual components.

message.content: the texts should be small just like how other apps like X(twitter), ig, gemini etc look like......is it like that

content.text:
Honestly, I can't confirm that without seeing it on a device — the change is correct in principle (14px is the base those apps use), but I can't visually verify it from here.

The best way to check: **open the dev server on your phone** (or use browser DevTools → mobile emulation) and compare side-by-side with Twitter or Instagram.

If it still feels too large, we can drop to `13px`. If `text-xs` labels (like timestamps, sub-labels) look too tiny, we can fine-tune those specifically. Let me know what you see.
Great, glad it works. Want to commit that and move on to the next thing?

content.text: Found the bug. The `themeMap` in `LivePreview.tsx` uses old keys (`dark`, `bronze`, `minimal`, etc.) but `LINK_THEMES` uses entirely different IDs (`elite_obsidian`, `pristine`, `exec_slate`, etc.). Every theme falls through to the dark fallback. I need to rewrite `LivePreview` to use the actual theme data from `LINK_THEMES`.

lastPrompt: the crevia link themes are not function when in live preview fix it. work on this first and dont do anything else....pls be aware of the tokens

content.content: (Bash completed with no output)

content.text:
Clean. The root cause was a complete mismatch between theme IDs — `LivePreview` had its own map with keys like `dark`, `bronze`, `sunset` while `LINK_THEMES` uses `elite_obsidian`, `pristine`, `exec_slate`, etc. Every theme silently fell through to the dark fallback.

**What changed:**
- `LivePreview` now imports `LINK_THEMES` directly and looks up the selected theme by ID
- Background and text colors are applied via inline `style` (the `previewBg`/`previewText` values from `linkThemes.ts`) instead of Tailwind classes that never matched
- Font map updated to cover `plus-jakarta` and `dm-serif` which are the keys the `ThemeSelector` actually sets

content.content:
src/components/crevia-link/LivePreview.tsx | 114 ++++++++++-------------------
 1 file changed, 38 insertions(+), 76 deletions(-)

input.command:
git add src/components/crevia-link/LivePreview.tsx && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Fix CreviaLink live preview not applying selected themes

LivePreview had its own themeMap with stale keys (dark, bronze, sunset)
that never matched the actual LINK_THEMES IDs (elite_obsidian, pristine,
exec_slate, etc.), causing every theme to silently fall back to dark.
Now imports LINK_THEMES directly and applies previewBg/previewText via
inline styles. Also adds plus-jakarta and dm-serif to the font map.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
